extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1, whose condition requires hyperkalemia AND low_ejection_fraction AND pregnancy AND peptic_ulcer AND polypharmacy. rec_drug_b is set true by r2 whenever hyperkalemia is true. Since r1's condition includes hyperkalemia as a conjunct, any input that fires r1 (a:=true) necessarily also fires r2 (b:=true). The two rules write to different outputs, so there is no priority conflict to suppress either — both apply. Thus the conjunction (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is unavoidably true whenever rec_drug_a is true. Setting all five conjuncts of r1 to true (hyperkalemia, low_ejection_fraction, pregnancy, peptic_ulcer, polypharmacy = true; prior_gi_bleed irrelevant, set false) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true, violating the mutual-exclusion property.
